name: Gemini 3 Pro
note: Google's frontier reasoning/agentic-coding flagship; sparse MoE, natively multimodal, 1M context; >200k-token prompts bill at $4/$18 per M.

results.notes: AIME 2025, no tools (100% with code execution)
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (45.8% with search+code exec)
MMMLU, multilingual Q&A [benchmarkSlug corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: mmlu -> mmmlu, same score]
LiveCodeBench Pro, Elo rating (not a 0-100 pass rate)
CharXiv Reasoning, no tools, information synthesis from complex charts.
FACTS Benchmark Suite (held-out internal grounding, parametric, MM and search retrieval benchmarks).

LMArena (Chatbot Arena) text-leaderboard Elo at launch: 'It tops the LMArena Leaderboard with a breakthrough score of 1501 Elo.' First model to cross 1500 Elo, per Google's official Nov 18 2025 launch post; corroborated independently by contemporaneous press coverage (VentureBeat, apiyi.com news summary).

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated 't2-bench' (tau2-bench) tool-use score (85.4%, itself the average of Retail/Airline/Telecom). Google's evals-methodology doc discloses the per-domain split: 'Gemini 3.0 Pro scores 85.3%, 73.0% and 98.0% on these categories respectively' for Retail, Airline, Telecom. The Telecom figure (98.0%) maps cleanly to the existing tau2-bench-telecom taxonomy slug; the aggregate 85.4% average across all three domains was NOT added since no general/average tau2-bench slug exists in the taxonomy.

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated τ2-bench score: 'Gemini 3.0 Pro scores 85.3%, 73.0% and 98.0% on these categories respectively' for Retail, Airline, Telecom. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated τ2-bench score, Airline domain (see tau2-bench-retail note for the same table). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Fills the missing 'livecodebench' core benchmark; not a duplicate of livecodebench-pro since the two are methodologically different metrics (0-100 pass rate vs Elo).

verificationNotes:
Directly downloaded and read the official Gemini 3 Pro Model Card PDF ('Model Release: November 2025'). Its evaluation table matches every claimed score digit-for-digit: GPQA 91.9, AIME 95.0 (100% with code execution), HLE 37.5 (45.8% with search+code exec), SWE-Bench 76.2, MMMU-Pro 81.0, MMMLU 91.8, LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2,439. Release date Nov 18, 2025 corroborated by blog.google, CNBC, and InfoQ coverage. Pricing $2/$12 (base tier) and $4/$18 (>200k tokens) confirmed on ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing (via the now-succeeding Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview page, which carries forward identical base-tier pricing). Model card explicitly names it a 'sparse mixture-of-experts (MoE) transformer-based model... with native multimodal support,' confirming archClass/archDisplay directly.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap sweep (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu): checked Google's official Gemini 3 Pro Model Card PDF and its companion 'Model Evaluation – Approach, Methodology & Results' PDF (deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3_pro_model_evaluation.pdf), Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/*, artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-pro), Vals AI (vals.ai/benchmarks/*, vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3-pro-preview), and Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/gemini-3-pro, ~26 evaluated benchmarks across agents/coding/math-science/multimodal/misc categories). MATH-500 (96.4) and plain LiveCodeBench (86.4) were added from Vals AI's independent leaderboards, both cross-checked against the model's $2/$12 per-M-token pricing to confirm correct attribution (full detail in each result's notes field). MMLU (plain) is a genuine absence: Google's own eval tables report MMMLU (multilingual) instead of plain MMLU; Artificial Analysis has no standalone MMLU page (404, superseded by MMLU-Pro); Vals AI's catalog lists only 'MMLU Pro', no plain MMLU; and Epoch AI does not track it — all consistent with plain MMLU being 'largely saturated at the frontier' and retired by trackers in favor of MMLU-Pro. HumanEval (plain) is likewise absent everywhere checked (Google, Artificial Analysis [404], Vals AI [not in catalog], Epoch AI [not tracked], llm-stats.com [no score]); Vals AI's own LiveCodeBench write-up states outright that 'benchmarks like HumanEval have become saturated,' matching this corpus's 'long-saturated at the frontier' framing for the slug. MMMU (plain) is also absent, with one notable wrinkle: Google reports MMMU-Pro (81.0%, already recorded in this file) rather than base MMMU; Artificial Analysis has no standalone MMMU page (404); and while Vals AI's UI labels one of its benchmark rows simply 'MMMU' (87.51% for this model), that page's own methodology section confirms it is actually run under the full MMMU-Pro protocol ('We adhered closely to the official MMMU Pro evaluation protocol,' 4-option format, ~1,700-question official MMMU-Pro dataset) — so it is a mislabeled MMMU-Pro data point, not evidence for plain MMMU, and was correctly excluded rather than used to fill this gap or duplicate the existing mmmu-pro entry. Epoch AI's multimodal category (VPCT, GeoBench, EnigmaEval) does not include MMMU either. All three omissions (mmlu, humaneval, mmmu) reflect genuine, cross-verified absences rather than guesses.

2026-07-17 round-22 deep second-pass sweep for mmlu/math/humaneval, restricted to source types NOT used in the 2026-07-16 sweep (which had exhausted official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, and BenchLM). Checked all 6 mandated new source types:

(1) Stanford HELM — Classic MMLU leaderboard (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest) and HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest) are both frozen circa late-2024/early-2025 (newest models present: GPT-4o, Llama 3.3, Gemini 2.0 Flash); confirmed via live DOM text-search (`document.body.innerText.includes('Gemini 3')` → false on both) that Gemini 3 Pro is absent entirely, so neither leaderboard could supply a plain-MMLU number. HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest) DOES include "Gemini 3 Pro (Preview)" but its 5-scenario 'Core Scenarios' battery is MMLU-Pro / GPQA / IFEval / WildBench / Omni-MATH only: Mean 0.799, MMLU-Pro (COT) 0.903, GPQA (COT) 0.803, IFEval 0.876, WildBench 0.859, Omni-MATH (Acc) 0.556. None of these five map to this corpus's plain 'mmlu', 'math', or 'humaneval' slugs (MMLU-Pro and Omni-MATH are distinct, harder benchmarks per this corpus's own existing mmlu/mmlu-pro slug separation; HumanEval isn't in HELM Capabilities' scenario set at all). Noted in passing for context only (not added, out of this round's scope): HELM's own harness scores this model's GPQA at 80.3%, well below the model card's self-reported 91.9% — illustrating that harness/prompting choices materially move scores, which is exactly why a HELM MMLU-Pro/Omni-MATH number cannot be treated as a stand-in for plain MMLU/MATH.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar — targeted searches ("Gemini 3 Pro" MMLU site:arxiv.org, MATH benchmark baseline comparison, HumanEval score) surfaced papers (e.g. an automated GenAI-survey paper, SOOHAK, LiveK12Bench) that cite this model only on MMLU-Pro (90.1%), Omni-MATH-style, or bespoke exam benchmarks as baselines — never on plain MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval with a number attributable to a specific paper/table. No qualifying arXiv citation found.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs — checked EvalPlus's live leaderboard (evalplus.github.io/leaderboard.html) directly via browser DOM inspection (not the static HTML the search snippets summarize): confirmed via targeted text search that the newest Gemini entries are "Gemini 1.5 Pro 002", "Gemini 1.5 Flash 002", "Gemini Pro 1.5", "Gemini Pro 1.0" — no Gemini 3-generation model has ever been added, so the "Gemini 3 Pro HumanEval+ 91.0%" figure several SEO aggregators attribute to EvalPlus is not actually on EvalPlus and appears fabricated. No lm-evaluation-harness results.json or other committed run log against gemini-3-pro-preview was located on GitHub.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators — OpenRouter's Gemini 3 Pro Preview pages (model page + /benchmarks) list only LMArena, GPQA Diamond, MathArena Apex, MMMU-Pro, Video-MMMU, SWE-Bench Verified, Terminal-Bench 2.0 (fetched directly; explicitly confirmed 'Popular benchmarks like MMLU, MATH, AIME, and HumanEval are not mentioned'). Scale AI SEAL only exposes an SWE-bench Pro score (43.3%) for this model. LiveBench.ai and LiveCodeBench use their own contamination-resistant problem sets, not MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. PapersWithCode's MATH and HumanEval leaderboard URLs both now 302-redirect to huggingface.co/papers/trending (PapersWithCode appears decommissioned/merged), so it could not be checked live. Nanonets' IDP leaderboard page for this model covers only OCR/document-extraction benchmarks (OlmOCR, OmniDocBench, IDP Core), no LLM knowledge/math/code scores.

(5) Non-English sources — targeted Chinese ("Gemini 3 Pro MMLU 谷歌 评测 跑分") and Korean ("Gemini 3 Pro MMLU 벤치마크 점수") searches surfaced only unofficial SEO/marketing mirror sites (gemini-pro-cn.com, gemini-cn.com). Directly fetched gemini-pro-cn.com/gemini-3-pro/benchmarks.html: its own footer discloses '本站为非官方资源站点' (this site is a non-official resource site), presents MMLU 92.3% / MATH 76.8% / HumanEval 89.7% with zero methodology or source attribution, and is otherwise a traffic-generation page promoting third-party mirror-access services (XSimpleChat, 火鸦Chat, GPTOKK) — treated as non-credible fabricated content and excluded. No legitimate Chinese/Korean/Japanese community-lab benchmark post independently testing Gemini 3 Pro on MMLU/MATH/HumanEval was found.

(6) Sibling models — Gemini 3.1 Pro's official model card (deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro) carries a comparison table with Gemini 3 Pro columns for HLE, ARC-AGI-2, GPQA Diamond, Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Verified, MMMU-Pro, and MMMLU only — confirmed directly, no MATH/HumanEval column exists, and plain MMLU is likewise absent (only MMMLU). Gemini 3 Deep Think's evals-methodology PDF (storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3_deep_think_model_evaluation.pdf, read via full PDF text/table extraction) tabulates 'Gemini 3 Pro Preview (Thinking High)' alongside Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.2 on ARC-AGI-2 (31.1%), HLE (37.5%/45.8%), MMMU-Pro (81.0%), IMO 2025 (14.3%), Codeforces Elo (2512), IPhO 2025 theory (76.3%), CMT-Benchmark (39.5%), and IChO 2025 theory (69.6%) — again, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval row anywhere in this table. Gemini 3 Flash's evals page reports only MMLU-Pro (89%) for the Flash tier, consistent with the rest of the family never reporting plain MMLU.

Additionally verified (and excluded) two aggregator sites that WebSearch's auto-summaries claimed had concrete MATH/HumanEval numbers: serenitiesai.com/benchmark/models/gemini-3-pro shows MATH 92.0% and HumanEval+ 91.0%, but DOM inspection of its 'Papers' citation links shows they point only to the generic benchmark-definition repos github.com/hendrycks/math and github.com/openai/grade-school-math — decorative links to the dataset itself, not to any actual Gemini-3-Pro evaluation run — so these numbers are unsourced and were not used.

Data-integrity note flagged for follow-up (not resolved in this round, since it requires re-touching an already-exhausted source type rather than one of the 6 new ones): this file's own 2026-07-16 verificationNotes text states 'MATH-500 (96.4) ... were added from Vals AI's independent leaderboards,' but no benchmarkSlug:'math' entry is actually present in this file's results array — the addition appears to have been written up but never landed (or was later dropped without a note update). A same-day recheck of vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3-pro-preview's live category list (CorpFin v2, MedCode, MedScribe, MortgageTax, ProofBench, SAGE, TaxEval v2, Vibe Code Bench v1.1, GPQA Diamond, IOI, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU Pro, MMMU, SWE-bench) no longer shows a MATH-500 category at all, so it could not be independently re-confirmed today either. Recommend a targeted Vals.ai re-check/backfill pass specifically for this.

Conclusion: after exhausting all 6 new source types, mmlu, math, and humaneval remain genuine, cross-verified absences for Gemini 3 Pro — consistent with the pattern already established in the 2026-07-16 sweep that this model family's self-reported and independently-tracked evals have shifted to MMLU-Pro / Omni-MATH / MathArena Apex / HumanEval+-adjacent successors rather than the original saturated MMLU/MATH/HumanEval benchmarks.
